mnemonics: 3 V's: Volume, Velocity, Variety - The core definition of Big Data.
Evolution Timeline: 2000s (Term Coined), 2010s (Hadoop & Tools), 2020s (AI/ML Integration) - to remember the key phases.
keyPoints: Big Data is defined by the three V's: Volume (large amounts of data), Velocity (fast generation and processing), and Variety (different data types, structured and unstructured).
The term 'Big Data' gained popularity in the early 2000s, but the concept of analyzing large datasets for insights has existed for decades.
The evolution was driven by the data explosion from the web, social media, and IoT devices, necessitating new tools like Hadoop and NoSQL databases to handle the scale and complexity.
commonMistakes: Thinking Big Data is only about the size (Volume) and ignoring the critical roles of Velocity and Variety.
Believing Big Data is a new concept from the 2000s, when its foundational ideas in data analysis are much older.
